name: Moving Platform
description: A surface moves through the world while transferring some or all of its motion to passengers standing on it.
design_notes: Moving platforms are mostly about trust at contact boundaries. The platform_velocity, contact_normal, passenger transfer mode, edge detach rule, and rollback snapshot state all need to agree so riders do not slide, jitter, clip, or lose control when the platform starts, stops, reverses, or changes parent space.
design_purpose: Create timed traversal routes without requiring every challenge to be a jump distance test.
Move players, objects, or hazards through authored spaces with predictable passenger behavior.
Support puzzles where carried momentum, route timing, or platform synchronization matters.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can ride, jump from, and trust moving surfaces without fighting the collision system.

parameters.name: platform_velocity_vector
parameters.typical_range: 0.5-20 world units per second along route
parameters.description: Current platform movement vector transferred to passengers according to the selected transfer rule.

parameters.name: route_node_id
parameters.description: Route, spline, or waypoint sequence used to compute the platform pose each fixed step.

parameters.name: passenger_transfer_mode_id
parameters.description: Policy for applying platform displacement to standing characters and carried objects.

parameters.name: contact_normal_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum upward contact normal required before an actor is treated as a platform passenger.

parameters.name: detach_grace_ms
parameters.description: Short grace window that preserves transferred motion after a rider steps or jumps off the platform.

edge_cases: The platform reverses direction while a passenger is partly off the edge or touching a wall.
A rider jumps on the same fixed step that the platform changes route node or teleports to a reset pose.
Parenting a character to a rotating platform changes input axes, camera assumptions, or interpolation order.
Rollback restores the player but not the platform route progress, producing a different contact frame.
common_bugs: Passenger velocity is applied twice: once through transform parenting and once through character movement.
Fast downward platforms leave riders behind because contact is lost before transfer occurs.
Moving platforms crush characters against ceilings without a clear damage, stop, or push-out rule.
Client interpolation smooths the platform but the character rides the unsmoothed server pose and jitters.
balancing_notes: Platform routes should telegraph start, stop, and reversal points early enough for players to plan jumps.
Fast platforms create excitement but reduce accessibility unless detach grace, coyote windows, and camera framing support them.
Passenger transfer should feel consistent across player, enemies, and puzzle objects unless the difference is visibly intentional.
accessibility_notes: Avoid requiring blind jumps from moving platforms; use framing, route previews, or repeatable rhythms.
Provide wider timing windows or alternate routes when moving platforms combine with instant-fail hazards.

implementation_notes.unity: Kinematic Rigidbody platforms should move through MovePosition or a fixed-step route driver, then expose platform_velocity_vector to CharacterController or custom motor code. Prefer one passenger transfer path, either parent transform or velocity injection, and snapshot route progress plus platform pose for rollback.
implementation_notes.godot: AnimatableBody2D, AnimatableBody3D, or CharacterBody platforms need deterministic route updates before passenger movement. Use contact normals to decide passenger ownership, and store route_node_id plus path progress for save/load so platforms do not desync from riders.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use movement components, timelines, or splines to drive platform transforms on the server. CharacterMovement has base movement support, but custom platforms should replicate base actor, relative location, velocity, and route progress to avoid client jitter.
implementation_notes.web: In Phaser, Matter.js, or custom collision, move platforms before resolving passengers and include platform delta in the actor sweep. Avoid mutating render-only transforms; rollback snapshots need platform pose, route progress, and passenger contact id.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses moving blocks and platforms as precision traversal elements.

example_games.title: Super Mario World
example_games.note: Uses moving platforms across many platforming routes.
